verification_gate: Every current and proposed redb record class has a justified use case, owner, lifetime, durability requirement, identity, synchronization edge, crash-replay rule, recovery source, observability signal, and explicit relationship to PostgreSQL and CodeDB; no existing durable use is silently reclassified.

proposed_classes: PGREDB-007 (shared redb read-view for LifeOS UI)
gate_result.geometry_engine_covered: folded into PGREDB-004 as the f32 distance-math compute role over cached vectors; not a distinct record class

per_class_clause_coverage.owner: present for all 7 (single-writer per class named)
per_class_clause_coverage.identity: present for all 7 (SHA-256 blob / envctl run keys / agent-local key / BLAKE3 hash / sequence+idempotency key / shared-file identity)
per_class_clause_coverage.synchronization_edge: present for all 7 (forward-only; no undirected dual-write)
per_class_clause_coverage.relationship_to_postgresql: present and explicit for all 7 (split out from the combined column to satisfy the gate literally)
per_class_clause_coverage.relationship_to_codedb: present and explicit for all 7 (PGREDB-001/006 CodeDB store/buffer; PGREDB-002 explicitly SEPARATE; PGREDB-003/004/005/007 explicitly None or payload-only)

no_existing_durable_use_silently_reclass.how: Both durable uses are reclassified to durable-TRANSITIONAL and recorded in generated/redb_use_case_matrix.json reclassification_ledger with target owner, migration mechanism, verified cutover proof gate, and a zero-loss guarantee (SHA-256 identity preserved). Each remains redb-durable until its PostgreSQL replacement passes a green live parity/promotion proof. The reclassification is documented, evidence-gated, and rollback-reversible — never silent, never assumed, never lossy.

store_001_alignment.matrix_settles_prior_pending: generated/pgauth_redb_usage_inventory.csv left all classifications 'pending STORE-001'; this matrix applies the now-decided STORE-001 model. redb = transient tier (buffer/scratchpad/WAL/dedup+geometry cache/read-view); PostgreSQL+RuVector = canonical durable owner; migration honored via verified SHA-256 parity cutover, never by freezing redb as durable.
store_001_alignment.prior_brief_correction_honored: The pre-decision claim that any redb->transient demotion is a downgrade 'forbidden by PGAUTH-002' is corrected: building to the intended spec is an upgrade; the guardrail is 'no unverified cutover', not 'redb stays canonical'.

open_dependencies_declared: blobstore_parity.rs green run for PGREDB-001 cutover
RuVector-in-PostgreSQL activation for PGREDB-004 semantic derivation

proof_summary: PGAUTH-002 bounds redb's use cases and authority under the settled STORE-001 tiered model. generated/redb_use_case_matrix.json enumerates the complete evidence-grounded universe of seven redb record classes — two current (PGREDB-001 CodeDB source_blobs via codedb_store_redb, PGREDB-002 envctl migration_db) and five proposed (PGREDB-003 agent scratchpad, PGREDB-004 embedding/dedup cache which also carries redb's f32 geometry-engine compute role, PGREDB-005 application-level WAL/outbox, PGREDB-006 intermediate ingestion buffer, PGREDB-007 shared redb read-view for the LifeOS UDS UI) — and assigns each exactly once the full clause set the gate demands: justified use case, single-writer owner, lifetime, durability requirement, identity key, forward-only synchronization edge, crash-replay rule, recovery source, observability signal, and separately stated explicit relationships to PostgreSQL and to CodeDB. The two currently-durable uses are NOT silently reclassified: PGREDB-001 becomes durable-transitional with PostgreSQL as the STORE-001 canonical target, migrating only via a green live crates/codedb_store_pg/tests/blobstore_parity.rs cutover under one preserved SHA-256 identity; PGREDB-002 splits into a PostgreSQL-bound durable audit history (forward promotion) and a redb-transient in-flight runtime state, both recovery-preserving. Both are recorded in an explicit reclassification_ledger with a zero-loss guarantee, satisfying the gate's no-silent-reclassification clause. The proposed classes map to redb's intended terminal roles per the Architecture Blueprint (scratchpad, disposable BLAKE3 dedup cache + geometry engine, subordinate application-level WAL, transient ingestion buffer, non-authoritative UI read-view), with PostgreSQL+RuVector as the single durable authority and no redb dual-writer. This matrix also settles the prior pgauth_redb_usage_inventory.csv 'pending STORE-001' column and honors STORE-001's correction of the earlier brief (building to intended specs is an upgrade; the guardrail is verified cutover, not frozen redb durability). No redb, PostgreSQL, runtime, or user-space state was mutated; this is planning classification only, held for STORE-001's gated cutover proofs.
